LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING ON PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO DISMISS INDIVIDUAL CLAIMS WITH PREJUDICE AND CLASS/REPRESENTATIVE PAGA CLAIMS WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE MATTER IS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF RON ROUSSELL'S MOTION TO DISMISS INDIVIDUAL CLAIMS WITH PREJUDICE AND CLASS/REPRESENTATIVE PAGA CLAIMS WITHOUT PREJUDICE IS OFF CALENDAR. SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN. CLERK: R. WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-04-2026 Continued To Oct-07-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Dismiss Individual Claims With Prejudice And Class/Representative Paga Claims Without Prejudice
Off Calendar May-19-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF RON ROUSSELL , ON BEHALF OF THE STATE OF CALIFORINA AND ALL AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEES MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Dismiss Individual Claims With Prejudice And Class/Representative Paga Claims Without Prejudice.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Jamie Serb, Esq., of Crosner Legal, PC, (866) 276-7637, for Plaintiff.
Daniel M. Bruggebrew, Esq., of Coblentz Patch Duffy & Bass LLP, (415) 391-4800, for Defendant.

The matter is Argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Ron Roussell's Motion To Dismiss Individual Claims With Prejudice And Class/Representative PAGA Claims Without Prejudice is OFF CALENDAR.

Plaintiff filed a class action in this court, CGC-24-614539. Plaintiff later filed the instant PAGA action involving substantially identical facts. Plaintiff plans to seek dismissal of both actions on the same grounds.. One bench officer should resolve the dismissal issue as to both cases. Plaintiff never filed a Notice of Related Case, nor does the docket indicate they have yet sought consolidation or coordination. Plaintiff is ordered to file a notice of related cases (as required by the rules) and pursue consolidation and/or coordination. Thus, the court will have the opportunity to ensure one bench officer resolves the dismissal issue as to both cases. This motion is off calendar, to be renewed once the above process has played out.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
